title: Missing authorization check on route handler
description: Route handler processes requests without verifying the user's authorization level. Any authenticated (or unauthenticated) user may access protected resources.

remediation.description: Add authentication and authorization middleware to all route handlers. Use role-based or attribute-based access control.

title: Insecure Direct Object Reference (IDOR)
description: Resource accessed by user-supplied ID without verifying ownership. Attacker can enumerate and access other users' resources by changing the ID parameter.

remediation.description: Always verify that the authenticated user owns or has permission to access the requested resource. Add ownership checks after fetching the resource.
remediation.code_example:
const resource = await Resource.findById(req.params.id);
if (resource.userId !== req.user.id) return res.status(403).json({ error: 'Forbidden' });

title: Path traversal in file access
description: User-supplied path used in file system operations without sanitization, allowing access to arbitrary files outside the intended directory.

remediation.description: Normalize the path and verify it stays within the allowed base directory. Use path.resolve() and check that the resolved path starts with the expected prefix.
remediation.code_example:
const safePath = path.resolve(BASE_DIR, req.params.filename);
if (!safePath.startsWith(BASE_DIR)) return res.status(400).send('Invalid path');

title: Hardcoded secret or API key in source code
description: Sensitive credentials (API keys, passwords, tokens) are hardcoded in the source code, risking exposure through version control or build artifacts.

remediation.description: Move all secrets to environment variables. Use a .env file (excluded from version control) or a secrets manager.
remediation.code_example:
const apiKey = process.env.API_KEY;
if (!apiKey) throw new Error('API_KEY environment variable is required');

title: Insecure cookie configuration
description: Session cookies set without HttpOnly, Secure, or SameSite flags, making them vulnerable to XSS theft or CSRF attacks.

remediation.description: Set HttpOnly, Secure, and SameSite flags on all session cookies.
remediation.code_example: res.cookie('session', token, { httpOnly: true, secure: true, sameSite: 'strict', maxAge: 3600000 });

title: Weak password hashing algorithm
description: Passwords hashed with MD5, SHA1, or SHA256 without proper salting.

remediation.description: Use bcrypt, argon2, or scrypt for password hashing.
remediation.code_example:
import bcrypt from 'bcrypt';
const hash = await bcrypt.hash(password, 12);

title: DOM-based XSS via innerHTML
description: User input directly inserted into DOM via innerHTML without sanitization, allowing script execution.

remediation.description: Use textContent instead of innerHTML. If HTML is required, sanitize with DOMPurify.

title: React unsafe HTML rendering prop
description: React component uses the dangerous HTML rendering prop, bypassing React's built-in XSS protection.

remediation.description: Avoid React's dangerous HTML prop. If necessary, sanitize the HTML with DOMPurify before passing it.

title: Vue v-html directive with unsanitized content
description: Vue's v-html directive renders raw HTML, bypassing automatic escaping.

remediation.description: Use v-text or {{ }} interpolation instead of v-html. If HTML rendering is required, sanitize with DOMPurify.

title: SQL injection via string concatenation
description: SQL query built with string concatenation using user input, allowing injection of arbitrary SQL.

remediation.description: Use parameterized queries or an ORM. Never concatenate user input into SQL strings.
remediation.code_example: const result = await db.query('SELECT * FROM users WHERE id = $1', [req.params.id]);

title: OS command injection via user input
description: User-controlled data passed to shell execution functions without sanitization.

remediation.description: Avoid passing user input to shell commands. Use execFile/spawn with arguments array (no shell interpretation).
remediation.code_example:
import { execFile } from 'child_process';
execFile('convert', ['-resize', '100x100', validatedFilePath], callback);

title: Dynamic code execution vulnerability
description: Dynamic code evaluation function used with potentially user-controlled input, allowing arbitrary code execution.

remediation.description: Replace dynamic code evaluation with safe alternatives. Use JSON.parse() for JSON data.

title: Missing CSRF protection on state-changing endpoint
description: POST/PUT/DELETE endpoint lacks CSRF token validation.

remediation.description: Implement CSRF protection using tokens or SameSite cookies. For APIs using Bearer tokens, CSRF is typically not needed.
remediation.code_example:
import csrf from 'csurf';
app.use(csrf({ cookie: { sameSite: 'strict', httpOnly: true } }));

title: Missing rate limiting on authentication endpoint
description: Login endpoint has no rate limiting, enabling brute-force attacks.

remediation.description: Add rate limiting to authentication endpoints.

title: Missing Content-Security-Policy header
description: Application does not set a CSP header.

remediation.description: Set a Content-Security-Policy header. Use helmet.js for Express apps.

title: Debug mode enabled in production configuration
description: Application configured with debug mode in production.

remediation.description: Ensure debug mode is disabled in production.

title: Permissive CORS configuration
description: CORS configured with wildcard origin or reflects request origin without validation.

remediation.description: Configure CORS with a specific allowlist of trusted origins.

title: JWT token stored in localStorage
description: JWT stored in localStorage, accessible to any JavaScript and vulnerable to XSS-based theft.

remediation.description: Store tokens in HttpOnly cookies instead of localStorage.

title: JWT without expiration or with excessive TTL
description: JWT tokens issued without expiration claim or with excessively long TTL.

remediation.description: Always set an expiration on JWT tokens. Use short-lived access tokens (15-60 minutes).

title: Insecure deserialization of user input
description: User input deserialized without validation, potentially allowing object injection or prototype pollution.

remediation.description: Validate all deserialized data against expected schemas.

title: Sensitive data in log output
description: Passwords, tokens, or sensitive data logged to console or files.

remediation.description: Never log sensitive data. Use structured logging with field-level redaction.

title: Server-Side Request Forgery via user-controlled URL
description: Server makes HTTP requests to a user-supplied URL without validation.

remediation.description: Validate and allowlist URLs before making server-side requests. Block private/internal IP ranges.
